refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize cycle time by optimizing load, travel, and dump phases to meet the customer-defined cycle time target.
refined_fr.rationale: Breaking down the overall cycle time KPI into the three main phases (load, travel, dump) allows targeted functional requirements that can be independently optimized and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure p95 cycle time for each phase and overall under representative duty cycles and site conditions; compare against CT_target.
sub_frs: Minimize load phase cycle time to meet target
Minimize travel phase cycle time to meet target
Minimize dump phase cycle time to meet target

candidate_dps: Optimize hydraulic system and bucket control for load phase
Optimize vehicle routing and speed control for travel phase
Optimize dump mechanism and positioning for dump phase
selected_dp.selected_dp: Optimize hydraulic system and bucket control for load phase

potential_sources: Load phase optimization affects travel start time
Travel phase speed affects dump timing
coupling_check.notes: Coupling between phases is sequential and manageable; each DP can be designed to minimize interference.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate sensor data for phase transitions
Risk of delays due to external site conditions not modeled
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit modeling of phase transition interfaces in next refinement.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components responsible for load phase including hydraulic system and bucket control

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Send load phase status and performance data to NGHE Solution

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive commands for load phase operations from NGHE Solution

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level coordination but limited direct control of load phase
Primary containment of load phase DP; low diffusion risk
Supports remote control but not primary for load phase
Monitors safety alerts but no direct load phase control

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Load phase DP is best contained within Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and enable focused optimization.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with NGHE Solution for tasking and telemetry
Risk of duplicated logic if Teleoperation also implements load control
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface contracts and ownership boundaries between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FRs are logically decomposed and candidate DPs address each sub-FR with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible duplication of load control logic between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment score and low diffusion risk for load phase DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and tasking command logs at Vehicle module level.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear ownership of load phase DP within Vehicle module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface protocols between Vehicle and Teleoperation to avoid duplication.
Expand refinement for travel and dump phases in next iteration.

decision.justification: While the load phase DP and IM assignment are sound, the overall cycle time KPI requires further decomposition into travel and dump phases to fully cover the requirement.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize travel phase cycle time to meet target
Minimize dump phase cycle time to meet target

revise.why: To decompose the cycle time KPI into atomic sub-FRs for travel and dump phases enabling focused DP and IM assignments.

assumptions: Phase transitions are well-defined and measurable
Vehicle module can fully contain load phase DP
open_risks: Interface complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
External site conditions impacting cycle time not fully modeled
evidence_needed: Telemetry data confirming phase timing accuracy
Interface specifications between Vehicle and Teleoperation
